learning_outcomes.description: Understand what material indices are and explain how Material Indices relevant to a variety of applications can be determined.
Create and use Materials Selection Charts.
Select materials that optimises performance in a given application while meeting external constraints.
Understand what a Shape Factor is and use Indices which include Shape Factors to rank candidate materials for a given application.
Identify Multiple Constraints and the active (i.e. the most restrictive) constraint in a given application and apply material selection method to these scenarios.
Understand what exchange constants and penalty functions are and apply trade-off analysis using Compound Objectives/Materials Substitution.
Select materials rationally to meet environmental objectives by identifying the phases of product life, energy consumption in production and manufacture, and ways of disposal.

assessments.task_description:
The question sheet will be published in Blackboard before the Applied Class. You will be solving the problem-based questions with the help of the teaching team. The answers will be published in Blackboard after the due day. 

A1: Use of Materials Selection Charts (weight: 5%)

A2: Material Indices (weight: 10%)

A3: Materials and Shape (weight: 10%)

A4: Multiple Constraints & conflicting objectives (weight: 10%)

A5: Hybrid Materials (weight: 10%)
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission of these assessment items must be through TurnItIn via Blackboard site to detect any possibilities of plagiarism or collusion with another student(s) or existing documents.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Practical and Report - Material Selection for Bicycle Forks
assessments.task_description:
Materials Selection Project completed in pairs. This assessment is a project-based report that requires students to attend a practical session to collect experimental data. Students will then required to analyse the data and prepare a report based on their findings.

Students who are unable to attend their allocated practical session should contact the teaching team to discuss the available options.

See details in Blackboard, Laboratory Project folder.

If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The Final Exam normally consists of a set of questions involving short derivations of material indices and the use of charts, and one or two exercises on Materials Selection related to existing components of, e.g., cars, aeroplanes, home appliances, bridges, musical instruments, sport goods, etc. (See examples of past exams at the library: https://www.library.uq.edu.au/exams/search.html).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
